name: Speed
description: How fast something is moving—distance divided by time

content.overview: Speed answers: **HOW FAST?** Your car going 60 km/h, a ball rolling at 5 m/s—that's speed. Speed tells you how much distance is covered in a given time.
content.explanation:
Speed = distance ÷ time

Speed is a SCALAR—just a number, no direction. "60 km/h" is a speed. "60 km/h north" would be velocity (next topic).

**Average speed vs instantaneous speed:**
- Average speed = total distance ÷ total time
- Instantaneous speed = speed at one exact moment (what your speedometer shows)

**Example:**
You drive 100 km in 2 hours.
- Average speed = 100 km ÷ 2 h = 50 km/h
- But you might have gone 60 km/h for part of it, 40 km/h for another part

**Units:**
- m/s (meters per second) - SI unit
- km/h (kilometers per hour) - common
- mph (miles per hour) - US/UK
- All are valid, just different scales

Speed is always positive. You can't have negative speed (unlike displacement or velocity).
keyPoints: Average speed: total distance ÷ total time
Instantaneous speed: speed at one moment
content.whyItMatters: Speed is how we quantify how fast something moves. Every time you see 'km/h' or 'm/s,' you're dealing with speed.
